name: Sudanese Arabic
notes: Sudanese Arabic — Khartoum/central Sudan koine, part of the Egypto-Sudanic group. DEFINING FEATURE: ج jim → /ɟ/ (voiced PALATAL stop) — an archaic reflex distinguishing Sudanese from both Egyptian (/ɡ/) and Levantine (/ʒ/); /dʒ/ also occurs. ق qaf → /ɡ/ (voiced velar stop, Bedouin-origin reflex). Interdentals MERGE (not retained): ث θ → /t/ (~/s/ in loans), ذ ð → /d/ (~/z/), ظ/ض → /dˤ/ (~/zˤ/) — shared Egypto-Sudanic innovation. Vowel system tends to lower/retract; /u/ and /i/ qualities differ from Egyptian. Source: Dickins (2007) Sudanese Arabic Phonematics and Syllable Structure; Holes (2004); Sudanese Arabic (Wikipedia).

sources.author: Dickins, J.
sources.title: Sudanese Arabic: Phonematics and Syllable Structure
sources.notes: Detailed phonological description: jim→ɟ, qaf→ɡ, interdental merger.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
